findings.detail: Diamond sits at bottom and branches into Left/Right, both converging on Base at top -- a genuine diamond/lattice shape, not a linear chain. Matches design intent for the multiple-inheritance / MRO topic.
'MRO order' ghost annotation reads 'Diamond, Left, Right, Base, object', which is an exact match for topic_corpus.md 5.3's printed Diamond.__mro__ tuple. Concept fidelity confirmed.
'super() -> next in MRO (Right, not Base)' label box sits cleanly in the open space between Left and Right nodes -- no overlap with either node box or with the nearby 'inherits' edge labels.

stats.title: Diamond MRO — super() Follows Computed Order, Not Literal Parent
stats.concept_fidelity: pass vs topic_corpus.md §5.3 (diamond problem, MRO, super() resolution order)
stats.label_discipline: pass — all node labels ≤2 words, within 4-word cap
stats.title_discipline: pass — governing concept in `title:` config field, not a dangling ghost node
stats.single_path_discipline: n/a — corpus §5.3 describes a genuinely branching diamond structure, not a linear flow
stats.mermaid_parse: not independently re-run (no shell/bash tool available in this session); source syntax manually reviewed and is well-formed standard flowchart syntax matching prior working examples in this repo

visual_checks.crossings_illegible: none found — Diamond->Left, Diamond->Right, Left->Base, Right->Base curves are visually distinct with no confusing overlaps
visual_checks.layout_wrong_for_intent: none found — BT diamond shape renders correctly as a branching lattice, not linear
visual_checks.whitespace_imbalance: minor asymmetry (bottom-right, near MRO annotation, is emptier than the main diamond column) but well under 40% of canvas — not a blocking issue
visual_checks.subtitle_illegible: none found — 11px italic subtitles are legible at 1400w viewer scale
visual_checks.color_tier_violation: none found — start (Diamond, entry), auto (Left/Right, intermediate classes), done (Base, terminal/base case), ghost (MRO annotation, no border) all applied consistently with project palette tiers
visual_checks.edge_label_collision: none found — both dotted-edge labels ('super() -> next in MRO (Right, not Base)' and '__mro__') sit in open space, clear of node boxes and of each other
